programmes.show_name: The Opening Track from Every NOW!
programmes.show_description: A look back at the opening tracks from all the '80s NOW albums - from Phil Collins on NOW 1 to Tears for Fears on NOW 16. Plus those TV adverts that made them famous!
Dramatic storylines, big dance routines, neon lights and iconic fashions - these are the classic music videos that made 1988!
Another top year, with rock, reggae, pop & power ballads rubbing shoulders with exciting new dance sounds from the US & Europe. Bruno Brookes has the story - month by month.
Dramatic storylines, big dance routines, neon lights and iconic fashions - these are the classic music videos that made 1988!
You may remember the artists, but do you recall their lesser heard songs? With Style Council, Boy George, Chris Rea, Billy Idol & Squeeze.
Sixty minutes of back to back UK chart runners-up from 1987, a year of big hits for Pepsi, Percy and the Pet Shop Boys! With Mr Charts himself, Mark Goodier.
Dramatic storylines, big dance routines, neon lights and iconic fashions - these are the classic music videos that made 1987!
What was in the top five during the Easter holiday in the '80s? We choose three mega-hits from each year, 1980 to 1989, with Madonna, David Bowie, Bros, The Jam, Culture Club and more!
All '80s - 24/7 - the feelgood pop decade where the Brits invaded the world!

programmes.show_name: As Seen on TV with Robin Banks
programmes.show_description: Robin Banks' pick of tracks with big TV connections! Could be theme tunes, adverts or just a song made famous by a TV show. 'Just Say Yes' to our top 25 chart!
They all had one defining chart smash, then never darkened our top 20 again! Here are 40 one hit wonders from Spagna, Kaoma, Robin Beck, Men at Work and many more.
All '80s - 24/7 - the feelgood pop decade where the Brits invaded the world!
They all had one defining chart smash - then never darkened our top 20 again! Our one-hit stars include Judy Boucher, The Vapors, Matthew Wilder and Midnight Oil.
You may remember the artists, but do you recall their lesser heard songs? With New Order, Queen, Depeche Mode & Janet Jackson.
A complete rundown of the 30 biggest selling UK singles for 1986 - can you believe these were all hits 40 years ago? And if you thought Madonna was at number 1 - think again!
You may remember the artists, but do you recall their lesser heard songs? With New Order, Queen, Depeche Mode & Janet Jackson.
Twelve artists that had a single big song in 1986 - then never darkened the UK Top 20 ever again! Remember Sly Fox, It Bites, Gregory Abbott or Modern Talking?
Mark Goodier with 1986's big music award winners. What was NME's 'Best Dance Record' and who was 'Best Male' at the Australian Pop Awards? All will be revealed!
Soundtrack songs from the original of 1986 and the sequel from 2022. Including The Who, OneRepublic, Lady Gaga, Kenny Loggins, Harold Faltermeyer and Berlin.

programmes.show_name: The Heritage Chart with Mike Read
programmes.show_description: Another episode of the Heritage Chart - new music by heritage acts! With Mike Read.
Popcorn at the ready, these are NOW 80s' biggest songs from the movies. But don't forget to switch off those mobile phones!
You may remember the artists, but do you recall their lesser heard songs? With Blancmange, Elton John, Ultravox, Malcolm McClaren & Bananarama.
Sixty minutes of back to back UK chart runners-up from 1984 - a year of Pointer Sisters, Weather Girls and Wild Boys! With Mr Charts himself, Mark Goodier.
Dramatic storylines, big dance routines, neon lights and iconic fashions - these are the classic music videos that made 1984!
3 hours, 3 decades, 33 classics - in order!
Who knows more about teen pin-ups than one of the 80s' finest? The singer of 1987's biggest pop anthem selects 19 other hotties who shared our bedroom wall!
A choice selection of the 191 singles that reached the top of the UK charts in the 1980s!
Pop groups ruled the '80s and they came in all flavours! From rockers and New Romantics at the start of the decade to high energy and soul at the end. This is our choice of the best 50!
Timmy's back with something a little different - a run through of his favourite '80s bands, many interviewed on his 'Timmy on the Tranny' radio show throughout the decade!
Before the CD, the perfect pop songs were spun by singers and bands on 7 inch vinyl. From 1980-1984 these were the classic UK hits that invaded the world!
From 1981's early hits through to 1989's classic 'Violator' album, here's a slice of anthemic Depeche Mode - in order.
24 years of rock classics from the biggest band of the era - Queen! From '73's Keep Yourself Alive to '96's Too Much Love Will Kill You - via a multitude of all-time rock anthems.
Before the CD, the perfect pop songs were spun by singers and bands on 7 inch vinyl. From 1980-1984 these were the classic UK hits that invaded the world!
